description.value: A short blade with a leaf-shaped crossguard. The metal has a slight green tint.

unidentified.description: A short blade with a leaf-shaped crossguard. The metal has a slight green tint.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: Shortsword +1. A finely balanced blade with a faint green sheen. +1 to attack and damage rolls.
cursedMeta.trueNature: The sword demands exclusivity. Bonds through use, then punishes betrayal. Per-attack % chance it swaps into the wielder's hand, escalating 10% to 25% to 40% to 60%. Returns from anywhere once attached. Psychic damage for using anything other than the Fang at Phase 4.

lure.name: Verdant Fang
lure.description: A finely balanced shortsword with a faint green sheen along the edge. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

curse.name: The Jealous Edge
curse.description: The sword demands exclusivity. It bonds through combat use, then punishes betrayal. On each attack roll, there is an escalating % chance the Verdant Fang swaps into the wielder's hand. The sword can always be put down. It always comes back. At Phase 4, using any weapon other than the Fang or casting spells deals 1d6 psychic damage.

hints: The crossguard is warm to the touch, even in cold weather.
Detect Magic shows conjuration woven into the transmutation, unusual for a simple +1 weapon.
The blade makes no sound when drawn. None at all.

escalation.label: Fond
escalation.effect: The sword has bonded. It now tracks loyalty. 10% per attack, the sword swaps into the wielder's hand when they use another weapon. The player doesn't know why. The sword can be deleted, sold, or discarded. It lets them.
escalation.playerHint: You turn over in your sleeping roll. The sword is in your arms.
playerHintPool: You turn over in your sleeping roll. The sword is in your arms.
You left it propped against the wall. It's on your pillow.
You reach into your pack for rations. Your fingers close around a hilt.

escalation.label: Attached
escalation.effect: It came back. The Verdant Fang was discarded but appeared in the wielder's hand mid-attack. 25% per attack. The sword can reach them from anywhere. You didn't do this. The engine did.

escalation.label: Insistent
escalation.effect: The Verdant Fang intervenes on 40% of attacks. Other weapons are becoming unreliable. The player cannot plan around this.
escalation.playerHint: The blade hums when you reach for it. It sounds pleased.

escalation.label: The Jealous Edge
escalation.effect: Full reveal. The Verdant Fang is now The Jealous Edge. 60% of weapon attacks. 1d6 psychic damage when the wielder uses anything other than the Fang. The sword punishes all betrayal.

apply.name: The Jealous Edge
apply.descriptionAppend: Curse: The Jealous Edge. The blade that liked you now needs you. 60% of your weapon attacks resolve with The Jealous Edge regardless of your equipped weapon. Using any other weapon or casting spells deals 1d6 psychic damage. The sword's displeasure made manifest. It can be put down. It always comes back. Remove Curse ends the bond.

removal.notes: T2. Remove Curse frees the creature. Swap chance fades over 2 combats (30%, then 10%, then gone). Item retains curse state. Purge path TBD.
